name: Vega de Guadix and Cerro del Humilladero Trail
name_original: Vega de Guadix y Cerro del Humilladero – sendero circular desde Guadix
description: Hike the moderate 5.4-mile Vega de Guadix and Cerro del Humilladero Trail, exploring unique badland forms and gullies in 2h 20m.
region_intro: Hiking around Guadix offers access to a distinctive landscape characterized by its "badlands" topography, featuring eroded rock formations, canyons, and gullies. This desert-like environment, part of the UNESCO Geopark of Granada, displays a spectrum of colors. The region is also bordered by imposing mountain ranges, including the Sierra Nevada, providing a dramatic backdrop and varied terrain for outdoor activities. Unique cave houses, carved into the earth, are integrated into many hiking routes, offering a blend of natural and cultural exploration. Best hiking trails in Guadix The most popular hiking route is Guadix – circular route through the city, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 19 minutes to complete. This route explores the city's historical and cultural landmarks, including the unique cave quarter. Another top favourite among local hikers is Vega de Guadix and Cerro del Humilladero Trail, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.7 km) path. This trail offers views across the fertile Vega de Guadix and ascends to Cerro del Humilladero. Local hikers also love the Cave Quarter, Guadix – Guadix Cave Quarter loop from Guadix, a 10.9 miles (17.6 km) trail leading through the distinctive cave house district, often completed in about 4 hours 48 minutes. Hiking around Guadix is defined by its unique badlands, ancient cave houses, and the surrounding mountain ranges. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy city strolls to more challenging routes through the desert landscape. The routes in Guadix are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Guadix's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Guadix?
A: Guadix offers a diverse network of over 35 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, with approximately 18 easy trails and 20 moderate options, ensuring there's something for everyone to explore the unique landscapes.

Q: What makes hiking in Guadix unique?
A: Hiking in Guadix is defined by its extraordinary "badlands" topography, featuring impressive eroded rock formations, canyons, and gullies displaying a spectrum of colors. The region is part of the UNESCO Geopark of Granada and also offers the unique experience of exploring ancient cave houses integrated into many routes, blending natural beauty with cultural heritage.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly hiking options in Guadix?
A: Yes, Guadix has numerous easy trails suitable for families and beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38259479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave town Guadix – Alcazaba of Guadix loop from Guadix&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.5 km route that takes you through the city's historical areas and past the Alcazaba, offering a gentle introduction to the region's charm.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions to see while hiking in Guadix?
A: While hiking in Guadix, you can encounter several remarkable natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4101272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert&lt;/a&gt;, with its colorful badlands and 'fairy chimneys,' is a highlight. You can also explore the broader &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4101927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granada Geopark&lt;/a&gt;, which showcases the region's unique geological formations, including impressive canyons like those found in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Coloraos Badlands&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Guadix?
A: Yes, many routes in Guadix are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40370316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave Quarter, Guadix – Roman Theatre of Guadix loop from Guadix&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 5 km trail that explores the distinctive cave house district and historical sites.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Guadix?
A: The trails in Guadix are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the unique badlands scenery, the fascinating cave houses, and the dramatic backdrop of the surrounding mountain ranges, which offer a blend of natural beauty and cultural exploration.

Q: Can I explore the famous cave houses on a hike?
A: Absolutely. Many hiking routes in Guadix are designed to showcase the unique cave houses. For a more extensive experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19347787" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave Quarter, Guadix – Guadix Cave Quarter loop from Guadix&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 17.6 km trail that immerses you in this distinctive residential area.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Guadix?
A: The best time to hike in Guadix is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities, allowing you to fully enjoy the unique landscapes without the intense heat of summer or the cooler, sometimes wet, conditions of winter.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic views of the badlands or city?
A: Yes, several viewpoints are integrated into hiking routes, offering stunning panoramic vistas. Miradores like Padre Poveda, Cerro de la Bala, and Guadix provide excellent opportunities to see the vast badlands topography and the unique cave house district from above.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: While Guadix primarily features easy to moderate trails, some routes offer longer distances and more varied terrain. The region is also surrounded by imposing mountain ranges like the Sierra Nevada and Sierra de Baza, which provide opportunities for more challenging and extended treks for experienced hikers seeking higher elevations and rugged landscapes.

Q: What kind of geological features can I expect to see on a hike in Guadix?
A: Guadix is renowned for its dramatic geological features. You'll encounter impressive badlands topography, characterized by deeply eroded rock formations, canyons, and gullies that display vibrant colors. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4101927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granada Geopark&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, showcasing a desert-like environment shaped by millennia of erosion, including unique 'fairy chimneys' and ancient riverbeds.

Q: Are there any historical or archaeological sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, Guadix is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4101272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert&lt;/a&gt; area is home to the Gorafe Megalithic Park, where you can find over 240 'dolmens' (ancient funeral chambers) hidden among the ravines. Hiking through this park offers a fascinating journey through thousands of years of human history, combining natural exploration with archaeological discovery.
tips: The path of the Cerro del Humilladero, marked out by the Guadix Town Hall, runs along traditional agricultural paths that bring us closer to the gullies and reveal the landscapes of the Vega de Guadix. Along the path you will also find some information panels that support the interpretation of the route.
Construction began in 1492 on the site of the former Moorish mosque. However, it took centuries to complete.

Guadix is located in the province of Granada on the banks of the Río Guadix.
Guadix is known for its cave districts made of soft tufa rock.
There were about 2000 dwelling caves.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: The Vega de Guadix and Cerro del Humilladero Trail is considered a moderate hike. While the paths are generally accessible, good fitness is recommended due to its length and elevation changes.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect?
faqs.answer: You'll hike primarily on traditional agricultural paths through the unique landscapes of the Vega de Guadix. Expect to see distinctive geological formations, including characteristic gullies and the famous 'badland forms' made of solid clay, sculpted by erosion. Information panels along the route help interpret the local surroundings.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail itself is named after the Cerro del Humilladero Trail. While the route focuses on natural and agricultural landscapes, the nearby town of Guadix offers attractions like the Guadix Cathedral (Cathedral of the Incarnation) and the unique Cave Quarter, Guadix, which are worth exploring before or after your hike.

faqs.question: Is the Vega de Guadix and Cerro del Humilladero Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock, and remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Guadix region experiences hot summers, so the best times to hike are during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the temperatures are milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities, and the landscape can be particularly vibrant.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit, and there are no entrance fees to hike the Vega de Guadix and Cerro del Humilladero Trail. It's a publicly accessible route designated by the Guadix Town Hall.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route originating from Guadix. You can typically find parking within the town of Guadix itself, and the trail is well-marked from there. Specific parking areas might be available near the starting points of the designated route within the town.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily following the 'Vega de Guadix y Cerro del Humilladero' route for 74% of its length, this trail also intersects with sections of 'El Camino Espiritual del Sur' and 'Camino Mozárabe. Almería-Granada'.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given it's a moderate hike, we recommend bringing plenty of water, especially during warmer months, comfortable hiking shoes, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and snacks. While there are information panels, a map or GPS device is always useful. There are no specific water sources on the trail itself.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for children or families?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and length of approximately 8.7 kilometers, the trail is best suited for families with older children who are accustomed to hiking. Younger children might find the duration and elevation changes challenging. Good fitness is recommended for all participants.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the trailhead in Guadix?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the trail starts and ends in Guadix, you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and other amenities within the town. It's a great opportunity to enjoy local cuisine before or after your hike.
